tossups.question: A story by this author asks “Who has not seen the door in the wall,” using a fire door between number 20 and number 21 to start a philosophical paragraph about how “History could always have been otherwise.” Phil and his unnamed wife take a taxi to the Royal Athena Sun hotel in a story by this author, which ends by confirming that their driver ran over a child. This author of “Winter Break” used the phrases “rejoice” and “fucking rejoice” in a story in which an (*) IRA operative shoots a politician. This author wrote “The Assassination of Margaret Thatcher.” In an interview, this author said that Thatcher had a similar contempt of history as the protagonist of this author’s novel The Mirror and the Light. For 10 points, name this author whose trilogy about Thomas Cromwell includes Wolf Hall.
tossups.question_sanitized: A story by this author asks "Who has not seen the door in the wall," using a fire door between number 20 and number 21 to start a philosophical paragraph about how "History could always have been otherwise." Phil and his unnamed wife take a taxi to the Royal Athena Sun hotel in a story by this author, which ends by confirming that their driver ran over a child. This author of "Winter Break" used the phrases "rejoice" and "fucking rejoice" in a story in which an (*) IRA operative shoots a politician. This author wrote "The Assassination of Margaret Thatcher." In an interview, this author said that Thatcher had a similar contempt of history as the protagonist of this author's novel The Mirror and the Light. For 10 points, name this author whose trilogy about Thomas Cromwell includes Wolf Hall.
tossups.answer: Hilary Mantel [or Hilary Mary Mantel; or Hilary Mary Thompson]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Hilary Mantel [or Hilary Mary Mantel; or Hilary Mary Thompson]

tossups.question: In a novel by this author, Father Angwin is assisted by the mysterious title curate in an English moor village. The semi-autobiographical character Frances Shore joins her husband in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, in this author's novel Eight Months on Ghazzah Street. In a story by this author of Fludd, the narrator mistakes an assassin for the "boiler man Duggan" but still lets him use her window for the title "Assassination of Margaret Thatcher." One of this author's protagonists is beaten as a child by Walter at the start of a novel that features the antagonistic bishop Stephen Gardiner. This author's novel The Mirror and the Light is the last in a trilogy that depicts characters such as Thomas Wolsey and Jane Seymour. For 10 points, name this British author who wrote about Thomas Cromwell in Bring Up The Bodies and Wolf Hall.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a novel by this author, Father Angwin is assisted by the mysterious title curate in an English moor village. One of this author's protagonists is beaten as a child by Walter at the start of a novel that features the antagonistic bishop Stephen Gardiner. This author's novel The Mirror and the Light is the last in a trilogy that depicts characters such as Thomas Wolsey and Jane Seymour. For 10 points, name this British author who wrote about Thomas Cromwell in Bring Up The Bodies and Wolf Hall.

tossups.question: In a story by this author, a resident of Jeddah, who imagines that her furniture is moving during the night, is tormented by an unwanted friendship with the Pakistani man Ijaz. This author’s time in Saudi Arabia inspired that short story, “Sorry to Disturb.” In a novel that opens in Putney as the protagonist is brutally beaten by his drunkard father Walter, this author created a protagonist who raises Rafe Sadler as his clerk and loses his family to sweating sickness. Her novel The Mirror and the Light will complete a trilogy that has won two Booker Prizes and draws from a cast of historical figures including Stephen Gardiner, Jane Seymour, Thomas Wolsey, and Henry VIII. For 10 points, what British writer portrayed the life of Thomas Cromwell in her novels Bring Up the Bodies and Wolf Hall?

For 10 points, what British writer portrayed the life of Thomas Cromwell in her novels Bring Up the Bodies and Wolf Hall?

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about depictions of Thomas Cromwell in modern British literature, for 10 points each.
parts: Cromwell is the protagonist of a trilogy of novels by this author, which begins with Wolf Hall and ends with 2020’s The Mirror & The Light.
Cromwell bribes Richard Rich to perjure himself and bring about the execution of Thomas More for treason in this play by Robert Bolt.

A similarly villainous depiction of Cromwell is the title character of Privy Seal, the second in this trilogy of historical novels by Ford Madox Ford, which fictionalizes the life of Henry VIII’s penultimate wife, Catherine Howard.

bonuses.leadin: Name these British female novelists who wrote inventive historical fiction, FTPE.
parts: This author just published Bring Up the Bodies as the second book in her trilogy about Thomas Cromwell that began with the Booker-winning novel Wolf Hall.

She offered a fictionalized version of E. Nesbit called Olive Wellwood in her 2009 book The Children’s Hour, and described academics uncovering a hidden romance between Victorian poets Christabel LaMotte and Randolph Henry Ash in Possession.
This author’s novels in historical settings include The Gate of Angels and The Beginning of Spring, but she may be best known for her final novel about the German poet Novalis titled The Blue Flower.
